epic.spec:
# Build TypeScript PR Babysitter

## Overview
Build `pr-babysit` as a native TypeScript product that replaces `agent-reviews` instead of wrapping it. The product has three surfaces over one shared GitHub review core: a human CLI, an agent-facing MCP stdio server, and a tiny `/babysit` skill wrapper that launches `pr-babysit watch`.

The repo is greenfield: there is no package manifest, source tree, docs, git metadata, or reusable application code. `codedb.snapshot` contains zero indexed files, so the implementation should optimize for a clean vertical slice rather than migration compatibility.

## Scope
In scope: strict TypeScript scaffold, GitHub review-thread core, CLI JSON commands, MCP request/response tools, local webhook watch loop with `gh webhook forward`, local agent runner, fail-closed commit/push loop, fixture tests, and setup/safety docs.

Out of scope for v1: production webhook hosting, multi-PR watch mode, remote HTTP MCP, GitHub App check-run writes, and any runtime dependency on `agent-reviews`.

## Architecture
```mermaid
flowchart TD
  Human[Human or /babysit] --> CLI[pr-babysit CLI]
  Agent[Agent MCP client] --> MCP[pr-babysit mcp]
  CLI --> Core[Shared GitHub review core]
  MCP --> Core
  Webhook[GitHub webhook via gh forward] --> Watch[watch event loop]
  Watch --> Runner[Local agent runner]
  Runner --> Core
  Core --> GQL[GitHub GraphQL reviewThreads]
  Core --> REST[GitHub REST issue comments and checks]
  Runner --> Git[Fail-closed git commit and push]
```

## Quick commands
```bash
pnpm lint
pnpm typecheck
pnpm test
pr-babysit reviews list OWNER/REPO#123 --json
pr-babysit reviews reply-and-resolve THREAD_ID --body "Fixed in abc123."
pr-babysit mcp
pr-babysit watch OWNER/REPO#123 --fixture testdata/webhooks/pull_request_review_comment.created.json
```

## Acceptance
- **R1:** `pr-babysit` is a native TypeScript CLI/MCP implementation with no runtime dependency on `agent-reviews`.
- **R2:** Inline review state comes from GitHub GraphQL `reviewThreads`; PR issue comments are modeled as a separate surface.
- **R3:** Automated thread closure always replies before resolving; if the reply fails, the thread remains unresolved.
- **R4:** MCP exposes request/response tools only; no MCP tool polls GitHub, watches events, runs arbitrary shell, commits, or pushes.
- **R5:** `watch` acknowledges webhooks immediately, dedupes by `X-GitHub-Delivery`, and debounces/single-flights by `repo/pr/headSha`.
- **R6:** Only the latest target PR head SHA may mutate GitHub review state or git state; stale runs abort or become read-only.
- **R7:** Commit/push fails closed on dirty tree, detached or mismatched branch, missing upstream, remote advancement, and push rejection.
- **R8:** `watch` exits when the target PR closes or merges.
- **R9:** `checks.list` is explicitly read-only and defines whether it returns check runs, commit statuses, or both.
- **R10:** First usable release includes README, MCP config example, auth notes, webhook-forwarding limits, git safety behavior, and the tiny `/babysit` wrapper contract.

## Early proof point
Task `fn-1-build-typescript-pr-babysitter.2` validates the core approach by listing unresolved review threads and performing reply-before-resolve through the shared GitHub review core without `agent-reviews`. If that cannot be made reliable, reconsider the GraphQL-first design before building the watch loop or MCP surface.

## Risks
- GitHub API boundaries are easy to blur: review threads, issue comments, and checks must stay separate in types and tools.
- `gh webhook forward` is dev/test only and one forwarder per repo/org; docs and errors need to make that limitation obvious.
- Always-push is sharp. The implementation must fail closed instead of relying on prompts or operator memory.
- The agent runner is the least specified seam. Keep it thin, file-based, and easy to swap while the shared core owns GitHub mutations.

## References
- `.flow/config.json:2` — Flow memory is enabled in this greenfield planning workspace.
- `.flow/config.json:5` — cross-epic plan sync is disabled, so keep this as one self-contained epic.
- `.flow/config.json:9` — review backend is unset; `pr-babysit` should become the review engine rather than a wrapper.
- `.flow/meta.json:2` — fresh Flow seed with `next_epic: 1`.
- GitHub GraphQL mutations: `addPullRequestReviewThreadReply`, `resolveReviewThread`, `unresolveReviewThread`.
- GitHub webhook best practices: return quickly, dedupe via `X-GitHub-Delivery`, and treat failed deliveries as non-automatic redelivery.
- MCP TypeScript SDK: use the production v1 line with stdio first; do not mix v2 pre-alpha package/import surfaces into v1.

## Open questions for implementation
- In v1, treat “slash commands” as the local `/babysit` skill wrapper only; GitHub issue-comment slash commands can be a later product decision.
- In v1, make `watch` single-repo, single-PR, single-process.
- Store false-positive decisions as GitHub thread replies, not local hidden state.
- Define “always push” as: push after a successful agent run when the agent committed or when the CLI created a commit; no empty commits by default.

## Requirement coverage
| Req | Description | Task(s) | Gap justification |
|-----|-------------|---------|-------------------|
| R1 | Native TypeScript implementation with no `agent-reviews` dependency | fn-1-build-typescript-pr-babysitter.1, fn-1-build-typescript-pr-babysitter.7 | — |
| R2 | GraphQL review threads and separate issue comments | fn-1-build-typescript-pr-babysitter.2, fn-1-build-typescript-pr-babysitter.4 | — |
| R3 | Reply before resolve | fn-1-build-typescript-pr-babysitter.2, fn-1-build-typescript-pr-babysitter.3, fn-1-build-typescript-pr-babysitter.4 | — |
| R4 | MCP request/response tools only | fn-1-build-typescript-pr-babysitter.4 | — |
| R5 | Webhook ack/dedupe/debounce/single-flight | fn-1-build-typescript-pr-babysitter.5 | — |
| R6 | Latest-head mutation guard | fn-1-build-typescript-pr-babysitter.5, fn-1-build-typescript-pr-babysitter.6 | — |
| R7 | Fail-closed commit/push | fn-1-build-typescript-pr-babysitter.6, fn-1-build-typescript-pr-babysitter.7 | — |
| R8 | Exit on PR close/merge | fn-1-build-typescript-pr-babysitter.5 | — |
| R9 | Read-only checks contract | fn-1-build-typescript-pr-babysitter.2, fn-1-build-typescript-pr-babysitter.4 | — |
| R10 | Docs and skill wrapper contract | fn-1-build-typescript-pr-babysitter.7 | — |

tasks.spec:
---
satisfies: [R1]
---

## Description
Bootstrap the greenfield project as a strict TypeScript package with one executable `pr-babysit` entrypoint and a small internal layout for shared core, CLI, MCP, webhooks, agents, tests, and docs.

**Size:** M
**Files:** `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `src/bin/pr-babysit.ts`, `src/core/`, `src/cli/`, `src/mcp/`, `src/webhooks/`, `src/agents/`, `testdata/`, `README.md`

## Approach
- Use a single-package layout first; do not introduce a monorepo until shared package boundaries are proven.
- Use strict TypeScript, explicit exports from concrete files, and no barrel exports.
- Add lint, typecheck, and Vitest scripts before feature code.
- Keep the CLI entrypoint thin so subcommands delegate into typed modules.

## Investigation targets
**Required** (read before coding):
- `.flow/config.json:1` — Flow settings for this planning workspace.
- `.flow/meta.json:1` — confirms this is a fresh Flow seed.

**Optional** (reference as needed):
- GitHub Actions or release tooling can be deferred because no CI exists yet.

## Key context
The current repo has no `package.json`, no `.git`, and an empty CodeDB snapshot. This is a clean bootstrap, not a migration.

## Acceptance
- [ ] `pnpm lint`, `pnpm typecheck`, and `pnpm test` exist and pass on the scaffold.
- [ ] `pr-babysit --help` runs through the built CLI entrypoint.
- [ ] The package has no runtime dependency on `agent-reviews`.
- [ ] The source layout has clear homes for core GitHub logic, CLI commands, MCP tools, webhook handling, and agent runner code.

## Done summary
Pending implementation.

## Evidence
Pending implementation.

---
satisfies: [R2, R3, R9]
---

## Description
Build the shared GitHub review core used by both CLI and MCP. It should authenticate via `GITHUB_TOKEN` or `gh auth token`, fetch PR context, list paginated review threads through GraphQL, keep PR issue comments separate, provide read-only checks data, and enforce reply-before-resolve.

**Size:** M
**Files:** `src/core/github/`, `src/core/review/`, `src/core/types.ts`, `src/core/auth.ts`, `src/core/github/*.test.ts`

## Approach
- Treat GraphQL `reviewThreads` as the inline-review source of truth.
- Use GitHub REST issue comments only for top-level PR conversation comments.
- Model checks as read-only in v1; define whether the returned view includes check runs, commit statuses, or both.
- Make `replyAndResolve` perform reply then resolve, with no resolve attempt when reply fails.

## Investigation targets
**Required** (read before coding):
- `.flow/specs/fn-1-build-typescript-pr-babysitter.md` — acceptance and API boundaries.

**Optional** (reference as needed):
- GitHub GraphQL docs for `reviewThreads`, `addPullRequestReviewThreadReply`, and `resolveReviewThread`.
- GitHub REST docs for issue comments and pull request review comments.

## Key context
Prior memory and research agree that live `pullRequest.reviewThreads` is the reliable blocker graph; `agent-reviews --unanswered` style aggregation is noisier and should not drive the core model.

## Acceptance
- [ ] `listReviewThreads` returns unresolved, resolved, outdated, bot/human, path, line, author, URL, and capability fields from paginated GraphQL data.
- [ ] `replyToThread`, `resolveThread`, and `replyAndResolve` use GraphQL thread mutations and surface permission failures clearly.
- [ ] `listIssueComments` and `replyToIssueComment` are separate from review-thread APIs.
- [ ] `listChecks` is read-only and its check/status coverage is documented in the returned type and tests.
- [ ] Unit tests cover pagination, malformed responses, auth failure, rate-limit-shaped errors, and reply-success/resolve-failure partial outcomes.

## Done summary
Pending implementation.

## Evidence
Pending implementation.

---
satisfies: [R3]
---

## Description
Expose stable human and agent-shell CLI commands over the shared review core: PR parsing, review thread listing, replies, resolution, reply-and-resolve, comments, and checks. Commands must be usable from normal terminals and from agents that prefer shell JSON over MCP.

**Size:** M
**Files:** `src/cli/commands/reviews.ts`, `src/cli/commands/comments.ts`, `src/cli/commands/checks.ts`, `src/cli/pr-target.ts`, `src/bin/pr-babysit.ts`, `src/cli/**/*.test.ts`

## Approach
- Support `OWNER/REPO#123`, PR URL, number-only from current git remote, and no-arg current-branch resolution where possible.
- Keep `--json` output compact and stable.
- Use non-zero exits and machine-readable error shapes for auth/API/parse failures.
- Preserve reply-before-resolve behavior by calling the shared core convenience path.

## Investigation targets
**Required** (read before coding):
- `.flow/specs/fn-1-build-typescript-pr-babysitter.md` — CLI contract and target parsing rules.
- `src/core/` — shared review core from task 2.

## Acceptance
- [ ] `pr-babysit reviews list OWNER/REPO#123 --json` returns compact JSON from the shared review core.
- [ ] `pr-babysit reviews reply-and-resolve THREAD_ID --body "..."` replies before resolving and reports partial failures.
- [ ] `comments list/reply` and `checks list --json` use the separate core surfaces.
- [ ] PR target parsing is covered for `OWNER/REPO#123`, URL, number-only, no arg, invalid, and ambiguous cases.

## Done summary
Pending implementation.

## Evidence
Pending implementation.

---
satisfies: [R2, R3, R4, R9]
---

## Description
Add `pr-babysit mcp` as a stdio MCP server exposing typed request/response tools over the shared review core. The MCP surface should help agents inspect and mutate GitHub review state without knowing GitHub GraphQL or shell command details.

**Size:** M
**Files:** `src/mcp/server.ts`, `src/mcp/tools/review.ts`, `src/mcp/tools/pr.ts`, `src/mcp/tools/comments.ts`, `src/mcp/tools/checks.ts`, `src/mcp/**/*.test.ts`

## Approach
- Use the production MCP TypeScript SDK v1 line and stdio transport first.
- Expose `pr.get_context`, `review.list_threads`, `review.reply`, `review.resolve_thread`, `review.reply_and_resolve`, `review.mark_false_positive`, `comments.list`, `comments.reply`, and `checks.list`.
- Do not expose watch, polling, arbitrary shell, commit, or push tools.
- Make `mark_false_positive` reply with the reason and then resolve; do not store hidden local state.

## Investigation targets
**Required** (read before coding):
- `.flow/specs/fn-1-build-typescript-pr-babysitter.md` — MCP contract and no-polling boundary.
- `src/core/` — shared review core from task 2.

**Optional** (reference as needed):
- MCP TypeScript SDK v1 server/tool documentation.

## Key context
Current MCP docs warn not to mix v1 production imports with v2 pre-alpha package surfaces. Keep this task pinned to one MCP SDK line.

## Acceptance
- [ ] An MCP inspector/client can call every listed tool and receive compact structured JSON.
- [ ] Tool inputs are schema-validated and tool outputs are stable for agents.
- [ ] No MCP tool can poll, watch, run arbitrary shell, commit, or push.
- [ ] `review.mark_false_positive` leaves visible GitHub thread context by replying with the reason before resolving.

## Done summary
Pending implementation.

## Evidence
Pending implementation.

---
satisfies: [R5, R6, R8]
---

## Description
Implement `pr-babysit watch` as the local event loop: webhook server, target-PR filtering, `gh webhook forward` child process management, delivery dedupe, debounce/single-flight queue, and close/merge shutdown.

**Size:** M
**Files:** `src/cli/commands/watch.ts`, `src/webhooks/server.ts`, `src/webhooks/normalize.ts`, `src/webhooks/filter.ts`, `src/webhooks/debounce.ts`, `testdata/webhooks/`, `src/webhooks/**/*.test.ts`

## Approach
- `POST /webhook` should return `202` immediately after validation and enqueue work.
- `GET /healthz` should expose simple local readiness.
- Dedupe by `X-GitHub-Delivery`; debounce by `repo/pr/headSha`.
- Subscribe to PR, review, review comment, review thread, issue comment, check, workflow, and status events, but process only the target PR.
- Start `gh webhook forward` when needed, detect missing `cli/gh-webhook`, report one-forwarder conflicts clearly, and kill the child process on shutdown.

## Investigation targets
**Required** (read before coding):
- `.flow/specs/fn-1-build-typescript-pr-babysitter.md` — watch contract and event list.
- `src/cli/commands/reviews.ts` — CLI command patterns from task 3.
- `src/mcp/server.ts` — MCP startup boundary from task 4.

**Optional** (reference as needed):
- GitHub webhook docs for `X-GitHub-Delivery`, signature validation, redelivery, and `pull_request_review_thread` payloads.
- GitHub CLI docs for `gh webhook forward`.

## Acceptance
- [ ] `POST /webhook` acknowledges quickly with `202` and queues exactly one run for a fixture delivery.
- [ ] Duplicate delivery IDs do not enqueue duplicate runs.
- [ ] Bursts for the same `repo/pr/headSha` collapse into one processing pass with follow-up handling.
- [ ] Events for other PRs are ignored.
- [ ] `watch` exits on target PR close/merge and cleans up the forwarder child process.

## Done summary
Pending implementation.

## Evidence
Pending implementation.

data.title: Add agent runner and stale-head guards

tasks.spec:
---
satisfies: [R6, R7]
---

## Description
Add the local agent runner and mutation guardrails. The watch loop should generate a bounded prompt/input bundle for `codex`, `claude`, or a custom command, stream output, let the agent use MCP or CLI tools, and prevent stale-head runs from mutating GitHub or git state.

**Size:** M
**Files:** `src/agents/runner.ts`, `src/agents/prompt.ts`, `src/agents/commands.ts`, `src/core/pr-state.ts`, `src/git/status.ts`, `src/cli/commands/watch.ts`, `src/agents/**/*.test.ts`

## Approach
- Auto-detect `claude` and `codex`, with `--cmd` as an explicit override.
- Write the agent prompt/input bundle to a file and pass the target PR, head SHA, and available MCP/CLI commands.
- The agent should never wait for future events; the outer event loop calls it again when webhooks arrive.
- Re-fetch PR head state before any mutation path; stale runs must abort or downgrade to read-only.

## Investigation targets
**Required** (read before coding):
- `.flow/specs/fn-1-build-typescript-pr-babysitter.md` — agent behavior contract.
- `src/webhooks/` — queued event/run model from task 5.
- `src/core/` — shared review and PR context APIs.

## Key context
Untrusted GitHub comments and diffs may influence agent prompts. Keep them as data inside a bounded prompt and force actual GitHub mutations through typed CLI/MCP/core calls.

## Acceptance
- [ ] `pr-babysit watch OWNER/REPO#123 --agent auto` detects an available agent or fails with a clear setup error.
- [ ] Agent output streams to the terminal and failures stop the run with actionable output.
- [ ] The prompt tells the agent not to poll GitHub and not to wait for future events.
- [ ] Before reply/resolve/commit/push, stale target head SHA is detected and mutation is blocked.

## Done summary
Pending implementation.

## Evidence
Pending implementation.

data.title: Harden commit push and docs wrapper

tasks.spec:
---
satisfies: [R1, R7, R10]
---

## Description
Finish the fail-closed git commit/push loop, document the operational contract, and add the tiny `/babysit` skill wrapper that delegates to `pr-babysit watch` without duplicating review logic.

**Size:** M
**Files:** `src/git/status.ts`, `src/git/commit.ts`, `src/git/push.ts`, `src/cli/commands/watch.ts`, `skills/babysit/SKILL.md`, `README.md`, `docs/mcp-config-examples.md`, `docs/auth.md`, `docs/operations/gh-webhook-forwarding.md`, `docs/safety/always-push.md`, `src/git/**/*.test.ts`

## Approach
- Refuse to start watch mode on a dirty tree unless the implementation has an explicit safe handoff model.
- Before push, verify branch/upstream, fetch remote state, block detached or mismatched branch, and stop on push rejection.
- If the agent already committed, push that commit; if the agent changed files only, create the default review-feedback commit; if nothing changed, do not create an empty commit.
- The `/babysit` skill should only run `pr-babysit watch "$ARGUMENTS"`; no Python script, no `agent-reviews`, and no embedded review handling.
- Docs are part of the safety boundary, not polish.

## Investigation targets
**Required** (read before coding):
- `.flow/specs/fn-1-build-typescript-pr-babysitter.md` — always-push and wrapper contract.
- `src/agents/runner.ts` — agent output and mutation handoff from task 6.
- `src/cli/commands/watch.ts` — watch loop integration point.

**Optional** (reference as needed):
- Git docs for `status`, `commit`, `push`, upstream detection, and fast-forward safety.

## Key context
The repository currently is not a git checkout, so product tests must simulate git hazards and the final end-to-end proof needs a real PR in a real git repo.

## Acceptance
- [ ] Dirty tree, detached branch, branch mismatch, missing upstream, remote advancement, and push rejection all stop with clear errors.
- [ ] Agent-edits-only produces `fix: address PR review feedback`; agent-already-committed pushes; no-change does not create an empty commit.
- [ ] README explains setup, auth, main CLI flow, MCP flow, and one review-thread example.
- [ ] Docs cover MCP config, auth resolution, `gh webhook forward` limitations, and always-push safety semantics.
- [ ] `skills/babysit/SKILL.md` is a thin launcher for `pr-babysit watch "$ARGUMENTS"` and contains no `agent-reviews` path.

## Done summary
Pending implementation.

## Evidence
Pending implementation.
